So don't make up an outlandish value, but all the stuff that you're about to see when I get to my bonuses are real. They're the real values. And what I say is if I were to sell you this, this is what you would pay for it. This is important for FTC reasons, by the way. The FTC, I'm not saying they're going to come crashing on you. They're not going to come crashing on anyone that I know of. That is all talk right now. But just in case, and I think it's a good policy to follow just from ethics is you've got, okay, the FTC is saying you can no longer say that the bonuses are included in your offer. In other words, if you have an offer that's $97, and you say, okay, so you're going to get $5,000 in bonuses. So in reality, this offer is $5,097, but you're only going to pay $97. That's not true, because you're giving them the $5,000. So the wording to get around that is to say if you were to buy these separately, it would cost $5,000. Or the value of this is $5,000. So you're getting the value of $5,997 for $97. It's just, again, wordsmithing. But in this case, it's a legal wordsmithing.
